<!--
Copyright (c) 2026 Dhruv Azad. All rights reserved.
Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Dhruv Azad
-->


---
title: "Root Finding"
page-layout: full
---

Root finding methods are numerical techniques for solving equations of the form

$$
f(x)=0.
$$

These methods are central in computational mathematics because many problems cannot be solved exactly with algebra alone. Instead, we approximate the solution using an iterative algorithm.

<div class="card-grid">

<div class="topic-card">
<h3><a href="Bisection-Method/index.qmd">Bisection Method</a></h3>
<p>
Repeatedly halves an interval $[a,b]$ where $f(a)$ and $f(b)$ have opposite signs.
This method is reliable and guaranteed to converge for continuous functions with a valid sign-changing interval.
</p>
</div>

<div class="topic-card">
<h3><a href="Fixed-Point/index.qmd">Fixed Point Iteration</a></h3>
<p>
Rewrites an equation as $x=g(x)$ and generates approximations using
$x_{n+1}=g(x_n)$.
</p>
</div>

<div class="topic-card">
<h3><a href="Newton/index.qmd">Newton's Method</a></h3>
<p>
Uses tangent lines and derivative information to generate rapidly converging approximations to roots of $f(x)=0$.
</p>
</div>

</div>
